content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward signals (ICM and RND) can be re-purposed to dynamically prioritize which agent exploration experiences are stored in a fixed-size MemoryBank, pruning low-novelty outdated memories to avoid working memory overload while preserving the agent's curious exploration drive. This creates a self-sustaining curiosity loop where intrinsic novelty signals double as memory curation criteria.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
